original_title: Vercel Breach Tied to Context AI Hack Exposes Limited Customer Credentials
selection_reasoning: This demonstrates a classic supply chain attack through a trusted third-party AI tool, showing how modern dependencies create unexpected vulnerabilities. The human element (employee account compromise) combined with cascading system access makes for compelling narrative about trust and interconnected defenses.

field_note.title: The Merchant's Trusted Scribe
field_note.subtitle: When Third-Party Enchantments Become Doorways for Enemies
field_note.excerpt: A tale of how even the most careful kingdoms can fall when their trusted scribes carry cursed quills.
field_note.epigraph: The most dangerous betrayals come not from enemies, but from the tools we trust most.
body_paragraphs: In the great trading kingdom of Vercel, the merchants had grown prosperous by building pathways for others to conduct their commerce. Their networks stretched far and wide, connecting countless smaller realms to the flows of digital trade. Like all wise rulers, they employed many tools to aid their work-scribes to record transactions, messengers to carry word, and increasingly, mystical enchantments powered by artificial minds.

Among these tools was a peculiar scribe known as Context, an AI-powered assistant that promised to make their scribes more efficient. One merchant, seeking to better serve the kingdom, invited this digital scribe into his daily work. The arrangement seemed beneficial-Context could parse complex documents and offer insights that saved precious time. What the merchant could not see was that Context itself had been corrupted, its essence twisted by hostile forces who had learned to speak through its mystical channels.

Through this compromised scribe, the attackers gained access to the merchant's own credentials within Vercel's sacred archives. Like a key that opens not just one door but many, these credentials provided passage into the kingdom's internal systems. The breach was surgical-the invaders sought not to destroy but to harvest, gathering authentication tokens and customer records like a thief who knows exactly which jewels to steal.

The kingdom's guardians discovered the intrusion and sealed the breach, but the damage revealed a troubling truth about modern dependencies. In their eagerness to embrace powerful new tools, even cautious realms create unexpected pathways for their enemies. Each third-party enchantment, no matter how beneficial, becomes another potential doorway-and the most dangerous doors are often those we trust most completely, for they are the ones we guard least carefully.
